instruction: Fix a segmentation fault bug in `read_file()` and `read_file_raw()` functions that occurs when passing an empty raw vector (e.g., `raw()`). The functions should handle this edge case gracefully instead of crashing R. For `read_file()`, return an empty string `""` when given an empty raw vector. For `read_file_raw()`, return an empty raw vector `raw()` when given an empty raw vector. Create a helper function `empty_raw()` to check for this condition, similar to the existing `empty_file()` helper.

fail_to_pass: read_file() does not crash with segmentation fault on empty raw vector
read_file_raw() does not crash with segmentation fault on empty raw vector
pass_to_pass: read_file returns "" on an empty file
read_file_raw returns raw() on an empty file
read_file works with normal file input
read_file_raw works with normal file input

key_changes: Create new helper function `empty_raw()` that checks `is.raw(x) && length(x) == 0`
Add `|| empty_raw(file)` condition to the early return check in `read_file()`
Add `|| empty_raw(file)` condition to the early return check in `read_file_raw()`
potential_pitfalls: Must check both `is.raw(x)` AND `length(x) == 0` - checking only one condition is insufficient
Return appropriate empty values: `""` for `read_file()`, `raw()` for `read_file_raw()`
Don't break existing `empty_file()` handling - use OR condition, not replacement
Non-empty raw vectors (e.g., `raw(1)`) should continue to work normally
